---
title: "Pipeline Tutorial"
---

> **Status**: Verified on H17 against the live Pipeline Console and current
> `scripts/auto_pipeline.py` / `scripts/batch_pipeline.py` behavior (2026-07-17)
> **See also**: [Pipeline Overview](pipeline-overview.qmd) |
> [Epoch QA](epoch-qa.qmd) |
> [Outputs and Artifacts](outputs-and-artifacts.qmd)

This tutorial takes one observing date from slow-vis UVH5 files to hourly-epoch
mosaics and forced-photometry products. Use the dashboard to understand state;
use the commands when you need a reproducible or scripted run.

::: {.callout-important}
Run pipeline commands on H17 from `/data/dsa110-continuum` with the CASA 6
Python environment. Do not use system `python3`.
:::

## 1. Open the Pipeline Console

Open the [live Pipeline Console](https://dsa110-continuum.jakobtfaber.com/pipeline).
Read-only monitoring is public. Launching or terminating work requires the
operator-managed control token; the token is never stored in this repository.

The coverage matrix has one row per observing date. Click a row to inspect it.

![Pipeline coverage matrix. Each colored segment represents one stage; the
numeric columns show raw completeness, converted Measurement Sets, calibration
tables, science tiles, mosaics, photometry, and the final verdict.](images/pipeline-tutorial/01-coverage-matrix.png){width=100%}

### Read the row before launching anything

| Column | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| **Raw** | Complete 16-subband groups when indexing is current; otherwise the number of files awaiting index reconciliation |
| **MS ready** | Converted base Measurement Sets divided by complete indexed groups |
| **Cal** | Available bandpass (`B`) and gain (`G`) tables |
| **Tiles** | Science images recorded by `.tile_checkpoint.json`, not the number of WSClean FITS byproducts |
| **Fail** | Recorded tile failures; `q` marks entries at the quarantine threshold |
| **Mosaics** | Hourly-epoch mosaics in the staging directory |
| **Phot** | Forced-photometry CSVs that passed or explicitly overrode QA |
| **Verdict** | `CLEAN`, `DEGRADED`, or `FAILED` from the run manifest |

The ten stage segments are, in order: raw intake, indexing, conversion,
flagging, calibration, imaging, mosaicking, QA, photometry, and archive.
Green is complete, amber is partial, red is failed or blocked, and gray is
waiting or unknown.

## 2. Inspect the stage walkthrough

Click the date. The walkthrough explains both the state and the evidence used
to derive it.

![Expanded walkthrough for 2026-07-15. Raw intake and indexing are complete,
conversion is partial, imaging and mosaicking completed, and strict QA blocked
photometry and archival.](images/pipeline-tutorial/02-stage-walkthrough.png){width=100%}

The example above is intentionally not a successful science product:

- 20 complete groups were indexed, but only 12 base MS products were present.
- Those 12 MS products produced 12 science tiles and one hourly-epoch mosaic.
- Gain calibration had too many flagged solutions, so the manifest was
  `DEGRADED`.
- Strict QA withheld forced photometry and blocked archival.

Flagging is labelled **inferred** because the current pipeline does not persist
a standalone flagging artifact. A tile reaching imaging proves that flagging
ran, but it does not provide an independent flagging-quality verdict.

## 3. Choose the appropriate run mode

### Full flow: raw data to science products

Use **Full flow · raw → science products** when the slow-vis UVH5 files are on
H17 but indexing or conversion is incomplete. This runs
`scripts/auto_pipeline.py`, which:

1. indexes the date's files in the pipeline SQLite database;
2. converts complete 16-subband groups that do not already have a base MS;
3. launches the strict-QA science batch; and
4. waits for the registered batch to finish.

Indexing and conversion are idempotent: existing records and base MS products
are skipped. Full flow is a real run and has no dry-run mode.

![Full-flow control. The date and flow are validated, retry behavior is fixed,
lenient QA is disabled, and the control token is required.](images/pipeline-tutorial/03-full-flow-control.png){width=100%}

Equivalent CLI:

```bash
cd /data/dsa110-continuum
PY=/opt/miniforge/envs/casa6/bin/python

$PY scripts/auto_pipeline.py --date 2026-07-15
```

The final JSON line reports the intake counts, converted/skipped groups, run ID,
and final batch status. An indexing or conversion failure prevents the science
batch from starting.

### Science batch: existing MS to products

Use **Science batch · MS → products** when conversion is already complete or
when you want a bounded rerun. Start with a dry run; it prints discovery,
calibration-table selection, checkpoint, quarantine, and rebuild decisions
without writing products.

```bash
cd /data/dsa110-continuum
PY=/opt/miniforge/envs/casa6/bin/python

$PY scripts/batch_pipeline.py \
  --date 2026-07-15 \
  --start-hour 11 --end-hour 12 \
  --dry-run \
  --quarantine-after-failures 3
```

If the plan is correct, remove `--dry-run` and use the production safety and
parallelism settings:

```bash
$PY scripts/batch_pipeline.py \
  --date 2026-07-15 \
  --start-hour 11 --end-hour 12 \
  --quarantine-after-failures 3 \
  --tile-timeout 1800 \
  --retry-failed \
  --photometry-workers 4 \
  --photometry-chunk-size 0
```

::: {.callout-warning}
QA is strict by default. Do not add `--lenient-qa` to a production run: it is a
diagnostic override that permits QA-failing fluxes to enter photometry output.
If `--cal-date` is needed, pass a bare date such as `2026-07-15`, not a full
timestamp.
:::

## 4. Monitor the job

Dashboard-launched work appears in **Jobs** with its state and log tail. A CLI
run writes the same operational evidence under the date's products directory.

While a run is active, distinguish these outcomes:

| State | Action |
|---|---|
| Conversion backlog decreases | Normal; refresh the date row |
| Tile failure count increases | Read the failure stage and log before retrying |
| A tile reaches three failures | It is quarantined on subsequent runs |
| Gaincal reports `LOW_SNR` | Inspect calibrator selection and the manifest; do not loosen the 30% gate merely to force acceptance |
| QA is `DEGRADED` | Open the gate reasons and mosaic; photometry/archive should remain blocked |
| Run exits zero and QA is `CLEAN` | Continue to science-product inspection |

Clear quarantine only after the underlying failure has been corrected. Clearing
the counter does not repair the data or calibration.

## 5. Inspect the science products

Open the [Science page](https://dsa110-continuum.jakobtfaber.com/science) after
the run. It shows hourly-epoch mosaics, QA state, image peak/RMS/dynamic range,
forced-photometry summaries, and variability ranked across stacked epochs.

![Science-products view with variability ranking, light curve, epoch mosaics,
QA badges, and forced-photometry summary.](images/pipeline-tutorial/04-science-products.png){width=100%}

For a selected date, verify all of the following:

1. the mosaic has a valid weight companion;
2. the QA badge is `PASS` / the run verdict is `CLEAN`;
3. the RMS and dynamic range are plausible for that epoch;
4. the forced-photometry CSV exists; and
5. the median catalog flux ratio and completeness agree with the QA report.

Do not infer science acceptance from a visually attractive mosaic alone. The QA
gate combines flux scale, source recovery, and noise.

## 6. Find the output files

Staging products are written first:

```text
/stage/dsa110-contimg/images/mosaic_YYYY-MM-DD/
  .tile_checkpoint.json
  YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS-image-pb.fits
  YYYY-MM-DDTHH00_mosaic.fits
  YYYY-MM-DDTHH00_mosaic.weights.fits
```

Run evidence and accepted science products are under:

```text
/data/dsa110-proc/products/mosaics/YYYY-MM-DD/
  run_<UTC>.log
  YYYY-MM-DD_manifest.json
  YYYY-MM-DD_run_summary.json
  run_report.md
  YYYY-MM-DDTHH00_mosaic.fits          # only when archive QA permits
  YYYY-MM-DDTHH00_mosaic.weights.fits
  YYYY-MM-DDTHH00_forced_phot.csv      # only when photometry QA permits
```

The manifest is the authoritative explanation of a degraded run. The tile
checkpoint is the authoritative count of completed science tiles.

## 7. Fast troubleshooting

| Symptom | First check | Correct response |
|---|---|---|
| Raw files but no index | Raw and Index stage details | Run full flow for the date |
| Indexed groups but MS backlog | Conversion stage | Let full flow convert missing complete groups |
| No B/G tables | Calibration stage and manifest provenance | Allow the acquisition chain to generate or borrow validated, strip-compatible tables; never use unknown tables silently |
| `CORRECTED_DATA` all zeros | Pipeline log | Treat as a hard calibration failure; do not fall back to raw `DATA` |
| Repeated tile failure | Failure stage, log, checkpoint | Fix the cause, then retry or clear quarantine |
| `LOW_SNR` gaincal | Selected calibrator tile and gain-table flag fraction | Verify tile/catalog selection before changing thresholds |
| QA blocks photometry | Manifest gate reasons and mosaic card | Keep strict QA; diagnose flux scale, completeness, or RMS |
| Mosaic exists but is not archived | Archive gate | Expected for QA `FAIL`; do not copy it into science products manually |

For threshold definitions and diagnostic interpretation, continue to
[Epoch QA](epoch-qa.qmd). For file schemas and artifact-level debugging, see
[Outputs and Artifacts](outputs-and-artifacts.qmd).
